attacking_style: Launches decisive solo attacks on final climb (1-3km from summit), particularly effective when positioned within striking distance of GC leaders. Shows opportunistic acceleration on flatter terrain when chasing bonuses or closing small GC gaps.
team_tactics: INEOS deploys him within dual GC protection structure, using domestiques like DENNIS for extended pacing from 50km+ out before his late accelerations. Benefits from teammate attacks (CARTHY, HAMILTON) to reach finale selections.
terrain_preference: Excels on sustained plateau climbs and climb-weighted ITTs where superior climbing compensates for slower flat speed. Struggles on explosive final 2km ramps where pure climbing power deficit emerges compared to elite GC contenders.
vulnerability: Poor positioning awareness on transitional stages leading to time-losing crashes (2023 Giro examples: 3.7km crash, wet descent abandonment from 3rd GC). Climbing deficit exposed on steep finishing gradients after matching pace on flatter sections.
key_patterns: Climb-weighted ITT pacing: slower flat splits compensated by superior climbing pace (2020 Giro win, 2023 career-best)
Aggressive bonus hunting within 15-20 seconds GC deficit, leading climbs to secure intermediate sprints before summit finishes
Vulnerable to crashes/positioning errors on non-mountain stages despite GC ambitions (3 documented incidents 2023 Giro)
Late-race opportunism: initiates winning moves but occasionally lacks final acceleration to hold off faster finishers
Form peaks during stage races: consecutive podiums/near-wins show sustained climbing across multi-day efforts
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Giro exit from 3rd GC via crash despite career-best ITT and matching Roglič/Thomas, suggesting physical peak undermined by tactical/positioning fragility
